# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a developer migrating from Git to Jujutsu (jj). You have an existing local Git repository at `/home/user/my-project`. Your task is to start using jj with this repository without breaking Git compatibility.

1. Navigate to `/home/user/my-project`.
2. Initialize a colocated jj repository so that jj and Git can be used together.
3. Create a new bookmark named `feature-x`.
4. Create a new file named `feature.txt` containing the text `Hello jj`.
5. Create a new jj commit (or describe the working copy) with the description `Add feature.txt`.

When finished, the repository should be a valid colocated jj/Git repo, the `feature-x` bookmark should exist and be exported to Git, and the commit should have the specified description and file.

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/my-project and verify Git/Jujutsu availability
Initialize colocated jj repository in /home/user/my-project
Create feature-x bookmark and add feature.txt with required contents
Create jj commit with description Add feature.txt and export bookmark to Git
Verify colocated jj/Git repo, bookmark export, and commit contents

input.command: pwd && ls -la "/home/user/my-project" && command -v jj && jj --version && command -v git && git --version

output.output:
/home/user/my-project

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/my-project and verify Git/Jujutsu availability
Initialize colocated jj repository in /home/user/my-project
Create feature-x bookmark and add feature.txt with required contents
Create jj commit with description Add feature.txt and export bookmark to Git

input.command: printf 'Hello jj\n' > feature.txt && jj bookmark create feature-x

output.output:
Created 1 bookmarks pointing to wzprqukq 85672747 feature-x | (no description set)
Warning: Name and email not configured. Until configured, your commits will be created with the empty identity, and can't be pushed to remotes.
Hint: To configure, run:
  jj config set --user user.name "Some One"
  jj config set --user user.email "someone@example.com"

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/my-project and verify Git/Jujutsu availability
Initialize colocated jj repository in /home/user/my-project
Create feature-x bookmark and add feature.txt with required contents
Create jj commit with description Add feature.txt and export bookmark to Git
Verify colocated jj/Git repo, bookmark export, and commit contents

input.command: jj describe -m "Add feature.txt" && jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ && jj git export

output.output:

Parent commit (@-)      : ypsoummp 14d944a2 main | Initial commit
Warning: Name and email not configured. Until configured, your commits will be created with the empty identity, and can't be pushed to remotes.
Hint: To configure, run:
  jj config set --user user.name "Some One"
  jj config set --user user.email "someone@example.com"
Nothing changed.
Nothing changed.

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/my-project and verify Git/Jujutsu availability
Initialize colocated jj repository in /home/user/my-project
Create feature-x bookmark and add feature.txt with required contents
Create jj commit with description Add feature.txt and export bookmark to Git
Verify colocated jj/Git repo, bookmark export, and commit contents

input.command: ls -la .jj && jj log -r 'feature-x|@|@-' --no-graph && jj bookmark list && jj status

output.output:

input.command:
git branch --list && git show-ref --heads --tags && printf '
--- feature.txt ---
' && cat feature.txt && printf '
--- git show HEAD:feature.txt ---
' && git show HEAD:feature.txt

git branch --list && printf '
--- show-ref heads ---
' && git show-ref --heads && printf '
--- feature.txt working tree ---
' && cat feature.txt && printf '
--- git show HEAD:feature.txt ---
' && git show HEAD:feature.txt
git status --short --branch && git rev-parse --verify HEAD && git branch -a
printf '%s\n' '--- feature.txt working tree ---' && cat feature.txt && printf '%s\n' '--- git show feature-x:feature.txt ---' && git show feature-x:feature.txt

output.output:
--- feature.txt working tree ---
Hello jj
--- git show feature-x:feature.txt ---
Hello jj

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/my-project and verify Git/Jujutsu availability
Initialize colocated jj repository in /home/user/my-project
Create feature-x bookmark and add feature.txt with required contents
Create jj commit with description Add feature.txt and export bookmark to Git
Verify colocated jj/Git repo, bookmark export, and commit contents

input.result:
Completed in /home/user/my-project.

What was done:
- Initialized a colocated Jujutsu repository with `jj git init --colocate`
- Created bookmark `feature-x`
- Added `feature.txt` containing `Hello jj`
- Described the working-copy commit as `Add feature.txt`
- Exported jj refs to Git with `jj git export`

Verification:
- `.jj/` exists, so the repo is now colocated and still Git-compatible
- `jj bookmark list` shows:
  - `feature-x: ... Add feature.txt`
  - `main: ... Initial commit`
- `git branch -a` shows `feature-x` alongside `main`, confirming export to Git
- `git show feature-x:feature.txt` returns `Hello jj`
- `jj log` shows the `feature-x` commit with description `Add feature.txt`

Note:
- Jujutsu warned that user name/email are not configured for jj-created commits. The commit/bookmark state requested by the task is in place, but configuring `jj config set --user user.name ...` and `jj config set --user user.email ...` is recommended before pushing with jj.

For help or feedback:
- Discord: https://getpochi.com/discord
- Bug reports: https://github.com/TabbyML/pochi/issues
